summary: Matched and linked the Kyasarin egg item unit by converting `src/melee/it/items/itkyasarinegg.c` from NonMatching to Matching, implementing its state table, spawn/init helpers, motion/collision callbacks, damage/contact handlers, and the supporting item-vars/attribute structs. decomp.dev reported 11 new matches for `main/melee/it/items/itkyasarinegg`, including +1756 bytes matched code, +2300 bytes linked code, and +88 bytes matched/linked data. The PR had no body and no line comments; it was approved with "Very nice!"

key_files.role: Main matching work: added `it_803F9328`, spawn/init routines, motion/collision callbacks, contact/damage callbacks, and inline shared damage logic.
Added prototypes and corrected callback return types for the matched Kyasarin egg implementation.
Added `kyasarinEgg` to the `Item` union for `xDD4_itemVar` access.

what_changed: `configure.py` now treats `melee/it/items/itkyasarinegg.c` as `Matching`, linking the newly matched item code into the build.
Added `itKyasarinEgg_ItemVars` with explicit padding and offsets through `ip+DF8`, plus `itKyasarinEggAttributes` fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, and `x10` in `itCommonItems.h`.
Added the `kyasarinEgg` member to the `Item` `xDD4_itemVar` union so code can reference `egg->xDD4_itemVar.kyasarinEgg.x20` instead of raw offsets.

Defined `ItemStateTable it_803F9328[]` with five state entries using `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion0_Coll`, `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion1_Phys/Coll`, `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Phys/Coll`, and `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion4_Anim`.
Implemented `it_802EFA44` to create item kind `0xEC`, set facing direction and collision facing direction, clear/set item flags and vars, and enter the egg init path.
Implemented `it_802EFB0C` to reset/init the egg, apply horizontal velocity from special attributes and facing direction, zero vertical/depth velocity, and switch to state 0.
Implemented bounce/transition helper `it_802EFCC0`, which switches to state 1, reverses X velocity, and applies Y velocity from `itKyasarinEggAttributes.x8`.
Implemented `it_802EFD84`, which calls several item cleanup/state helpers, zeroes X/Y velocity, marks `kyasarinEgg.x20 = 1`, and switches to state 4.

Added shared inline contact logic `it_damage_inline` and routed `it_2725_Logic28_DmgDealt`, `it_2725_Logic28_Clanked`, `it_2725_Logic28_HitShield`, `it_2725_Logic28_Reflected`, and `it_802F022C` through it.

The shared contact logic handles the already-settled `x20` guard, non-motion-3 bounce behavior, effect spawn `efSync_Spawn(0x4d0, ...)`, `Item_8026AE84`, random branch `HSD_Randi(ap->x10)`, `it_802EFD84`, and `it_8026F3D4`.

Updated header signatures for matching-sensitive callbacks: `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion1_Coll` became `int`, `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Coll` became `int`, and `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion4_Anim` became `bool`.
smart_moves: Used an `inline bool it_damage_inline(Item_GObj*)` helper so five externally visible contact callbacks could share identical logic while still producing separate matched functions; decomp.dev reported each of those wrappers as a new 100% match.
Added small `PAD_STACK` annotations (`0x50` in `it_802EFA44`, `0x8` in the contact wrappers) to satisfy stack-layout matching requirements.
Matched both code and data by adding the `ItemStateTable` in the C file and then flipping the build config to `Object(Matching, ...)` only after `.data` and `.sdata2` also matched.

Typed the item-var and special-attribute accesses before implementing behavior, making offset-sensitive code such as `kyasarinEgg.x20`, `ap->x4`, `ap->x8`, `ap->xC`, and `ap->x10` readable and reusable.
Preserved unidentified address-based names for functions whose semantics were not established, avoiding overconfident renaming while still progressing the match.
decomp_lessons: For item object files, matching often requires coordinating three layers at once: the C implementation, the exported header prototypes, and `configure.py`'s Matching/NonMatching object status.
Item state-table data can be a matching blocker; reconstructing `ItemStateTable` entries with exact motion IDs and callback pointers can unlock `.data` matches as well as code matches.

When several item event callbacks compile to the same body, an inline helper plus tiny `PAD_STACK` wrappers can be an effective way to match repeated compiler output without duplicating hand-written code.
Adding item-specific structs to `itCommonItems.h` and the `Item` union in `types.h` is preferable to raw offset access when the layout is known enough to name fields and padding.
Callback return types are matching-sensitive. This PR changed some prototypes from `void`/`s32` to `int`/`bool` to fit the matched implementation and state-table usage.
Evidence for intent is limited because the PR body was empty and there were no line review comments; conclusions here are based mainly on the diff and the decomp.dev match report.
naming_conventions: State/motion callbacks use the existing `itKyasarinegg_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll}` pattern.
Logic28 callbacks use the existing item-specific `itKyasarinEgg_Logic28_*` pattern for named events such as `PickedUp`, `Dropped`, `Thrown`, and `ShieldBounced`.
Still-unknown functions remain address-named, including `it_802EFA44`, `it_802EFB0C`, `it_802EFCC0`, `it_802EFD84`, `it_802F022C`, and `it_802F0320`.
The item state table remains address-named as `it_803F9328`.
Item-specific structs use `itKyasarinEgg_ItemVars` and `itKyasarinEggAttributes`; the item union member uses lower camel-case `kyasarinEgg`.
Struct comments include both local field offsets and item-pointer-relative offsets such as `ip+DD4`, `ip+DF4`, and `ip+DF8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(0x50)` was used in the spawn function `it_802EFA44`; `PAD_STACK(0x8)` was used in the small damage/contact wrappers.
`inline bool it_damage_inline` was used to reproduce repeated contact-handler bodies for `DmgDealt`, `Clanked`, `HitShield`, `Reflected`, and `it_802F022C`.
Exact state-table construction was necessary: entries use motion IDs `0`, `-1`, `-1`, `1`, and `2` with specific anim/phys/coll callback slots.
Facing direction is converted to a signed collision direction (`-1` when `facing_dir == -1.0f`, otherwise `1`) before calling `mpCollSetFacingDir`.
Bounce behavior matches by reversing `egg->x40_vel.x` and loading `egg->x40_vel.y` from `itKyasarinEggAttributes.x8`.
Randomized hit behavior uses `HSD_Randi(ap->x10) == 0` to choose between `it_802EFD84` and `it_8026F3D4`.
The source contains a local comment questioning `Item_8026AE84` inlining (`//why is this being inlined?`), which is useful evidence that inlining behavior was part of the match puzzle.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 11 new matches for `main/melee/it/items/itkyasarinegg`: +1756 matched code bytes, +2300 linked code bytes, and +88 matched/linked data bytes.
Approved with the review text: "Very nice!"
No PR body and no line review comments were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `it_802EFA44`, `it_802EFB0C`, `it_802EFCC0`, `it_802EFD84`, or `it_802F022C`.
Search for later uses of `itKyasarinEggAttributes.x0/x4/x8/xC/x10` and `itKyasarinEgg_ItemVars.x24` to recover field meanings.
Compare other item `Logic28` contact handlers that use an inline helper plus `PAD_STACK(0x8)` wrappers.
Investigate whether the `//why is this being inlined?` comment around `Item_8026AE84` was cleaned up or explained in a later matching PR.
